title: Scapular Stabilization Exercises for Post-Stroke Shoulder-Hand Syndrome
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2j4ds","text":"This protocol describes a quasi-experimental study evaluates the effectiveness of a 4-week scapular stabilization exercise program for post-stroke patients with shoulder-hand syndrome. Using Pressure Pain Thresholds (PPT), Modified Ashworth Scale (MAS), Shoulder Pain and Disability Index (SPDI), and Constant Murley Score (CMS) the study aims to improve shoulder function, reduce pain, and enhance upper-limb recovery, offering evidence-based rehabilitation guidance. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"a2mg9","text":"To ensure precise execution of the quasi-experimental study evaluating scapular stabilization exercises for post-stroke shoulder-hand syndrome (SHS), the following guidelines must be followed:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"m01q","text":"Participant Screening:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5crj","text":"Confirm eligibility: post-stroke patients with clinically diagnosed SHS, able to follow instructions, medical stable.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1lp3b","text":"Exclude patients with severe shoulder instability, fractures, uncontrolled cardiovascular conditions, or other contraindications to exercise.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7mne9","text":"Training:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vmph","text":"Ensure physiotherapists delivering exercises are trained in scapular stabilization techniques and post-stroke rehabilitation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"edvrp","text":"Assessors must be trained in administering VAS, PPT, MAS, SPADI, and CMS reliably.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qfmr","text":"Outcome Assessments:  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2s9sq","text":"Measure pain (VAS, PPT), muscle tone (MAS), and shoulder function (SPADI, CMS) at baseline,  and post-intervention.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ar1j","text":"Assessments performed by blinded evaluators to minimize bias.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mkl","text":"Data Management:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6a9k8","text":"Collect data using structured forms and anonymize patient identifiers.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"el6tg","text":"Store data securely in password-protected database; conduct weekly audits to ensure accuracy and completeness.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7bi2q","text":"Safety and Ethics:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1568m","text":"Monitor patients for adverse events during sessions; adjust intensity or stop exercises as needed.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"egfvj","text":"Conduct study in accordance with institutional ethical guidelines and the Declaration of Helsinki.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"64mf2","text":"Obtain informed consent and respect participants’ rights to withdraw at any time.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c4540","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning:
{"blocks":[{"key":"8nq01","text":"Medical Contraindications: Do not perform exercises if the patient has severe shoulder instability, recent fractures, uncontrolled hypertension, cardiovascular issues, or other conditions that may be worsened by movement.
Pain and Discomfort: Stop exercises immediately if the patient experiences sharp pain, excessive fatigue, dizziness, numbness, or abnormal swelling.
Compensatory Movements: Monitor for improper scapular or shoulder movements; incorrect technique may cause strain or injury.
Supervision Required: Exercises should always be supervised by a trained physiotherapist, especially during initial sessions.
Home Practice: Ensure patients understand correct technique; improper self-administration may lead to injury.
Emergency Preparedness: First-aid equipment and emergency contacts must be available during all supervised sessions.
Individualization: Adjust intensity and repetitions based on patient tolerance.
